qas.q: 1. Prevention of sexual harassment (SH) and sexual assault (SA) in the Army is a __________ effort that starts with __________.
2. List some actions that you might take to help prevent SH/SA:
qas.a: Up to individual attendees (they should put those items that are most meaningful or applicable to them)

a. Notice the event
b. Interpret the event as a problem
c. Accept responsibility for doing something
d. Decide how to intervene (3Ds)
e. Take Action

a. Direct
b. Distract
c. Delegate

qas.q: 5. Using Personal Courage, what can you do to intervene and prevent SH and SA in the Army?
6. Sexual Harassment involves conduct that involves and is perceived as:
qas.a:
a. Unwelcome sexual comments/gestures
b. Hostile or offensive by a reasonable person

qas.q: 7. Army policy on sexual harassment applies to Soldiers 24/7 and on- or off-post.
8. The three categories of sexual harassment:
qas.a:
a. Verbal
b. Non-verbal
c. Physical contact

qas.q: 9. The two types of sexual harassment:
qas.a:
a. Quid pro quo
b. Hostile environment

qas.q: 10. In addition to submitting Anonymous and Formal Complaints to address sexual harassment charges, there are three types of informal complaint procedures which include:
qas.a:
a. Direct approach
b. Third-party
c. Chain-of-command

qas.q: 11. SA is a crime that affects individuals but not the overall Army due to its readiness and resiliency.
12. A person who is asleep, unconscious, or incompetent (incapable of consenting due to intoxication) cannot consent to sex.
13. SA is punishable under which laws :
qas.a:
a. Army Soldiers under the UCMJ
b. Army Civilians under Federal / State law

qas.q: 14. A freely given agreement to sex by a competent person is:
15. The two types of sexual assault reporting options for Army Soldiers:
qas.a:
a. Restricted reporting
b. Unrestricted reporting

a. Adverse/unfavorable personnel action
b. Ostracism
c. Acts of cruelty, oppression, & maltreatment
